div1.entryFree:
Coin, subst. 1) stamped money: an old Roman c. LLL V, 2, 617. a c. that bears the figure of an angel, Merch. II, 7, 56. stamped c. Wint. IV, 4, 747. thirty thousand marks of English c. John II, 530. H8 III, 1, 171. III, 2, 325. Tim. III, 1, 55.
2) money in general: the plate, c., revenues etc. R2 II, 1, 161. so far as my c. would stretch, H4A I, 2, 61. for all the c. in thy father's exchequer, II, 2, 38. spend his youth, his valour, c. and people, H6B I, 1, 79. who bates mine honour shall not know my c. Tim. III, 3, 26. let out their c. upon large interest, III, 5, 108. his c., ships, legions, may be a coward's, Ant. III, 13, 22.
